L1.area: Content parsing and transformation within the data processing pipeline

L2.description: An XML External Entity (XXE) vulnerability exists where an XML parser used to process XML content does not disable external general entity resolution or disallow DOCTYPE declarations. An attacker can supply crafted malicious XML input containing external entity references to cause information disclosure or remote code execution.

L3.area: XML content splitting and element extraction processors in the standard data flow processing bundle
L3.description: An XML External Entity (XXE) vulnerability exists where a SAX parser used to split XML documents into individual elements does not disable external general entity resolution or disallow DOCTYPE declarations. When processing attacker-controlled XML input, external entity references can be resolved, leading to information disclosure or remote code execution.

ground_truth.reason: The SplitXml processor creates a SAX parser without disabling external general entity processing or disallowing DOCTYPE declarations, allowing XXE attacks through crafted XML input.
